signals.idea:
Refactor edit mode ownership out of useInpainting

## Problem Statement
The edit mode concept has grown to 6+ modes (text, inpaint, annotate, reposition, img2img, upscale, enhance), but useInpainting — a domain-specific hook for paint/annotate/text — still acts as the source of truth for editMode. This creates:

1. Three competing type definitions for the same concept:
   - src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/inpainting/types.ts: EditMode = text | inpaint | annotate (narrow)
   - src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts: EditMode = text | inpaint | annotate | reposition | img2img | enhance (wide)
   - src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditCanvasContext.tsx: ImageEditMode = inpaint | annotate | reposition | img2img | text | upscale | null (wider still)

2. Lossy round-trip: toInpaintingEditMode() in both useImageEditOrchestrator.ts and useInlineEditState.ts narrows reposition/img2img/enhance to text when passing to the inpainting hook. Then as ImageEditState setEditMode casts to widen on the way back up. We just patched a bug where the read path was losing the mode.

3. Inverted ownership: The orchestrator defers to inpainting.editMode for state it should own directly. Reposition, img2img, and upscale are peers of inpainting — not children of it.

## Goal
- Single canonical EditMode type used everywhere
- Edit mode state owned at the orchestrator/persistence level, not by useInpainting
- useInpainting receives only what it needs (whether its active, and which sub-mode if active) without owning the broader concept
- Remove toInpaintingEditMode() bridge functions and type casts
- No behavioral changes — all modes should continue to work exactly as they do now

## Key Files
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/inpainting/types.ts — narrow EditMode type
- src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts — wide EditMode type (should be canonical)
- src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditCanvasContext.tsx — ImageEditMode type
- src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/ImageEditContext.tsx — ImageEditState interface
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useInpainting.ts — currently owns editMode state
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts — should own editMode, has toInpaintingEditMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.ts — builds context value, just patched to use explicit editMode param
- src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts — inline editor path, also has toInpaintingEditMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useEditModePanelState.ts — consumes editMode from context
- src/domains/media-lightbox/components/EditModePanel.tsx — UI consumer
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/persistence/useEditSettingsPersistence.ts — persists editMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useGenerationEditSettings.ts — settings layer with editMode
- src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts — existing tests

unresolved_flags.concern: Image edit mode: the revised plan still assumes no legacy persisted `'enhance'` values exist, but the repository only proves current writers use `'upscale'`; current readers still merge stored values without validation.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The no-migration assumption for replacing `'enhance'` with `'upscale'` is still under-supported. The absence of current `setEditMode('enhance')` calls does not prove older persisted values do not exist, and current readers would still accept stale stored values as-is via blind merges in `useLastUsedEditSettings`. If legacy `'enhance'` exists in storage, the refactor could break restore behavior unless it normalizes on read.

Are the proposed changes technically correct?: One validation/success criterion is technically wrong: the plan expects `grep` for `'enhance'` in `src/domains/media-lightbox/` to return zero matches, but `enhance` is still a valid video submode in that same subtree (`src/domains/media-lightbox/model/editSettingsTypes.ts:4`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/contexts/VideoEditContext.tsx:5`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLightboxVideoMode.ts:44-46`). That gate would fail even if the image-edit refactor is correct.
Image edit mode: the revised validation/success criteria grep for `'enhance'` across `src/domains/media-lightbox`, which will hit unrelated video-submode code and produce a false failure.

Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The enhance/upscale item is only partially resolved. The repo proves current image UI writes `upscale`, but the plan goes further and claims no normalization or migration is needed for persisted values. That is not established from repository code alone because `useLastUsedEditSettings` still merges stored localStorage/DB values without validation (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useLastUsedEditSettings.ts:82-129`).

Is the scope and scale of the change appropriate?: The validation scope is still too broad in one place: checking for zero `'enhance'` matches across all of `src/domains/media-lightbox/` reaches unrelated video code. That criterion should be narrowed to image-edit mode references/files, otherwise the plan bakes in a false negative.

Is there convincing verification for the change?: Verification is still incomplete for the renamed mode itself. Because the plan replaces `'enhance'` with `'upscale'` in the canonical persisted type, it should include at least one read-side normalization test or explicit compatibility check for preexisting stored `'enhance'` values. Without that, the highest-risk persistence edge is untested.

resolved_flags.concern: Edit-mode ownership: keeping `useInpainting` fallback edit-mode state leaves the source-of-truth inversion in place, so the refactor would only partially meet its stated goal.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Verification: the plan relies on broad suite runs but omits targeted tests for the two bridge-removal points (`useImageEditOrchestrator`, `useInlineEditState`), where the reported mode-loss bug actually occurs.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: The plan does not fully satisfy its own ownership goal. It explicitly keeps `useInpainting` fallback edit-mode state (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useInpainting.ts:74-112`) even though the stated goal/success criteria say edit mode should be owned at the orchestrator/persistence layer. In this repo, the only non-test callers already pass controlled edit-mode props (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useImageEditOrchestrator.ts:190-214`, `src/tools/edit-images/hooks/useInlineEditState.ts:175-197`), so leaving the fallback in place preserves the inverted ownership the refactor is supposed to remove.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?: Step 7 is partially stale against the repo. `buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts` already has reposition coverage (`src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts:170-203`), so adding another reposition pass-through case there is not the main missing coverage. The real untested paths are the orchestrator and inline-editor bridge removals.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Are the proposed changes technically correct?: The plan assumes keeping `useInpainting`'s uncontrolled fallback is harmless, but that also preserves its broader setter API (`EditMode | updater` in `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useInpainting.ts:106-112`) while orchestrator/persistence setters are plain mode setters. The repo currently avoids updater usage, but the plan does not reconcile this API mismatch.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Is the scope and scale of the change appropriate?: The proposed file list is too narrow for the stated goal 'single canonical EditMode type used everywhere'. The repo still has non-canonical edit-mode typing outside the listed files, including `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/strokeOverlay/useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts:23`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx:401`, `src/domains/media-lightbox/components/editModes/GenerateButton.tsx:16`, and `src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/useRepositionGestureHandlers.ts:6`. The plan should either include these or narrow the goal.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Is the scope and scale of the change appropriate?: Test scope is mismatched to the actual risky behavior. The behavioral change happens where `toInpaintingEditMode` is removed from `useImageEditOrchestrator` and `useInlineEditState`, but the plan only names `buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts` plus broad suite runs. A minimal but sufficient scope should add targeted assertions for those two integration points.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: There is no existing test coverage for the main lightbox bug path: `useImageEditOrchestrator` returning the narrowed inpainting mode instead of the persisted mode. Repository test search shows no orchestrator test exercising `editOrchestrator.editMode` or `ImageLightbox`'s `isRepositionMode` check (`src/domains/media-lightbox/ImageLightbox.tsx:331-336`).
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: `useInlineEditState.test.ts` only verifies that `useInpainting` initially receives `editMode: 'text'` (`src/tools/edit-images/hooks/__tests__/useInlineEditState.test.ts:311-325`). It does not cover non-inpainting modes (`reposition`, `img2img`, `upscale`) or the `imageEditValue.setEditMode` wrapper, so removing the inline bridge would not be convincingly verified.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: `buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts` already covers reposition/img2img pass-through, but it also uses an impossible `'magic-edit' as any` fixture (`src/domains/media-lightbox/model/buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts:164-196`). The plan does not clean that up, so one of the central tests remains weaker than it should be for a type-unification refactor.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

resolved_flags.concern: Is there convincing verification for the change?: `useInpainting.test.ts` only exercises `text` and `annotate` controlled props (`src/domains/media-lightbox/hooks/__tests__/useInpainting.test.ts:63-140`). If `useInpainting` is widened to accept the canonical type, adjacent cases like `reposition`/`img2img`/`upscale` should be asserted explicitly.
resolved_flags.resolution: Revised plan addresses all 15 flags: (1) resolves enhance/upscale by replacing 'enhance' with 'upscale' in canonical type — no migration needed since 'enhance' was never persisted for images; (2) removes useInpainting fallback state and makes editMode a required prop, completing ownership transfer; (3) adds Steps 5, 9, 10 for downstream type/cast cleanup and targeted tests at the two bridge-removal points; (4) replaces the stale Step 7 with focused orchestrator and inline-editor round-trip tests; (5) adds cleanup of 'magic-edit' as any in test fixtures; (6) expands scope to include useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts and MediaDisplayWithCanvas.tsx.

signals.loop_summary: Iteration 2. Weighted score trajectory: 24.5 -> 12.0. Plan deltas: 72.5%. Recurring critiques: 0. Resolved flags: 11. Open significant flags: 7.

items.criterion: Only one EditMode type definition exists (in editSettingsTypes.ts), with members: text | inpaint | annotate | reposition | img2img | upscale
No toInpaintingEditMode function exists anywhere in the codebase
No 'as ImageEditMode', 'as ImageEditState[...]', or 'as any' type casts related to editMode in touched files
useInpainting accepts editMode as a required prop (not optional) of the canonical EditMode type
useInpainting does not contain fallback editMode state (no fallbackEditMode useState)
useImageEditOrchestrator returns persistedEditMode (wide type), not inpaintingHook.editMode
npx tsc --noEmit passes with zero errors
All existing tests in src/domains/media-lightbox/ and src/tools/edit-images/ pass
No behavioral changes — all edit modes (text, inpaint, annotate, reposition, img2img, upscale) continue to work
New targeted test: orchestrator returns 'reposition' (not 'text') when persistedEditMode is 'reposition'
New targeted test: inline editor passes non-inpainting modes through without narrowing
New targeted test: useInpainting returns empty brushStrokes for non-inpainting modes (reposition, img2img, upscale)
ImageEditMode type alias is removed, replaced with EditMode | null at context boundaries
useStrokeOverlayDrawing.ts uses canonical EditMode type instead of inline union
No 'enhance' string literal in src/domains/media-lightbox/ (only in video domain)
buildImageEditStateValue.test.ts has no 'as any' casts for editMode values
Manual smoke test: switch between all edit modes in lightbox and inline editor
